static char __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_assemble_stiffness_matrix[] = " Assemble a stiffness matrix from the coefficients of the differential equation.\n    \n    For the mathematics/definitions of the :math:`C` terms, see :ref:`FEM`.\n    All arguments should be defined on the mid mesh except :math:`U`,\n    and all the :math:`C` matrices should be three-dimensional, even if they are just scalars.\n    \n    If `Cl` and `Cr` are supplied as None, they will be ignored and the matrix will be of dtype double.\n    If `Cl` and `Cr` are supplied, the matrix will be of dtype complex.\n    \n    Args:\n        C0,Cl,Cr,C2: material-dependent differential equation coefficients\n        dzp: the spacings between mesh nodes\n        dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.\n    \n    Returns:\n        A stiffness matrix in sparse CSC form\n    ";
static PyMethodDef __pyx_mdef_9pynitride_4core_3fem_1assemble_stiffness_matrix = {"assemble_stiffness_matrix", (PyCFunction)(void*)(PyCFunctionWithKeywords)__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_1assemble_stiffness_matrix, METH_VARARGS|METH_KEYWORDS, __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_assemble_stiffness_matrix};

 028:     """ Assemble a stiffness matrix from the coefficients of the differential equation.
 029:     
 030:     For the mathematics/definitions of the :math:`C` terms, see :ref:`FEM`.
 031:     All arguments should be defined on the mid mesh except :math:`U`,
 032:     and all the :math:`C` matrices should be three-dimensional, even if they are just scalars.
 033:     
 034:     If `Cl` and `Cr` are supplied as None, they will be ignored and the matrix will be of dtype double.
 035:     If `Cl` and `Cr` are supplied, the matrix will be of dtype complex.
 036:     
 037:     Args:
 038:         C0,Cl,Cr,C2: material-dependent differential equation coefficients
 039:         dzp: the spacings between mesh nodes
 040:         dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.
 041:     
 042:     Returns:
 043:         A stiffness matrix in sparse CSC form

/* Python wrapper */
static PyObject *__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_3assemble_load_matrix(PyObject *__pyx_self, PyObject *__pyx_args, PyObject *__pyx_kwds); /*proto*/
static char __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_2assemble_load_matrix[] = " Assemble a load matrix from the coefficients of the differential equation.\n    \n    For the mathematics/definitions of the :math:`w` term, see :ref:`FEM`.\n    All arguments should be defined on the mid mesh, and the :math:`w` matrix should be one-dimensional floats\n    \n    Args:\n        w: load-side coefficient\n        dzp: the spacings between mesh nodes\n        n: the size of the :math:`C` matrices (eg 1 for scalar equation, 6 for 6x6 kp equation)\n        dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.\n    \n    Returns:\n        A load matrix in sparse CSC form\n    ";
static PyMethodDef __pyx_mdef_9pynitride_4core_3fem_3assemble_load_matrix = {"assemble_load_matrix", (PyCFunction)(void*)(PyCFunctionWithKeywords)__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_3assemble_load_matrix, METH_VARARGS|METH_KEYWORDS, __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_2assemble_load_matrix};

 110:     r""" Assemble a load matrix from the coefficients of the differential equation.
 111:     
 112:     For the mathematics/definitions of the :math:`w` term, see :ref:`FEM`.
 113:     All arguments should be defined on the mid mesh, and the :math:`w` matrix should be one-dimensional floats
 114:     
 115:     Args:
 116:         w: load-side coefficient
 117:         dzp: the spacings between mesh nodes
 118:         n: the size of the :math:`C` matrices (eg 1 for scalar equation, 6 for 6x6 kp equation)
 119:         dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.
 120:     
 121:     Returns:

+158: def fem_eigsh(stiffness_matrix,load_matrix,

static PyObject *__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_5fem_eigsh(PyObject *__pyx_self, PyObject *__pyx_args, PyObject *__pyx_kwds); /*proto*/
static char __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_4fem_eigsh[] = " Solve the eigenvalue problem.\n\n    For the mathematics/definitions of terms, see :ref:`FEM`.\n\n    Args:\n        stiffness_matrix: a stiffness matrix :math:`A` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_stiffness_matrix`\n        load_matrix: a load matrix :math:`M` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_load_matrix`\n        eigval_out: an array into which to fill the eigenvalues, should be shape (number of eigenvalues)\n        eigvec_out: an array into which to fill the eigenvectors, should be shape (number of eigenvalues x n x len(zp))\n        n: dimension of the differential equation\n        dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.\n        pairwise_GS: in the case of near degeneracy, the eigensolver often returns eigenvectors which are not totally\n            orthogonal.  For instance in a 6x6 wurtzite k.p problem with small CR coupling term, the states form pairs\n            that are very close in energy.  This option breaks the eigenvectors into those pairs [note, it just assumes\n            the pairing 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, etc, it doesn't actually check the energies], and in each pair, performs a single\n            Gram-Schmidt to ensure the nearly-degenerate eigenvectors are orthogonal.\n        ascending (bool): by default, energies are sorted from min to max, but for valence band problems,\n            you want the highest absolute energies first, so set `ascending=False`\n        *args,**kwargs: passed onto\n            `scipy.sparse.eigsh <https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh.html>`_\n            along with the main arguments `A` (stiffness) and `M` (load)\n\n    Returns:\n        None\n    ";
static PyMethodDef __pyx_mdef_9pynitride_4core_3fem_5fem_eigsh = {"fem_eigsh", (PyCFunction)(void*)(PyCFunctionWithKeywords)__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_5fem_eigsh, METH_VARARGS|METH_KEYWORDS, __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_4fem_eigsh};

 161:     """ Solve the eigenvalue problem.
 162: 
 163:     For the mathematics/definitions of terms, see :ref:`FEM`.
 164: 
 165:     Args:
 166:         stiffness_matrix: a stiffness matrix :math:`A` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_stiffness_matrix`
 167:         load_matrix: a load matrix :math:`M` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_load_matrix`
 168:         eigval_out: an array into which to fill the eigenvalues, should be shape (number of eigenvalues)
 169:         eigvec_out: an array into which to fill the eigenvectors, should be shape (number of eigenvalues x n x len(zp))
 170:         n: dimension of the differential equation
 171:         dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.
 172:         pairwise_GS: in the case of near degeneracy, the eigensolver often returns eigenvectors which are not totally
 173:             orthogonal.  For instance in a 6x6 wurtzite k.p problem with small CR coupling term, the states form pairs
 174:             that are very close in energy.  This option breaks the eigenvectors into those pairs [note, it just assumes
 175:             the pairing 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, etc, it doesn't actually check the energies], and in each pair, performs a single
 176:             Gram-Schmidt to ensure the nearly-degenerate eigenvectors are orthogonal.
 177:         ascending (bool): by default, energies are sorted from min to max, but for valence band problems,
 178:             you want the highest absolute energies first, so set `ascending=False`
 179:         *args,**kwargs: passed onto
 180:             `scipy.sparse.eigsh <https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh.html>`_
 181:             along with the main arguments `A` (stiffness) and `M` (load)
 182: 
 183:     Returns:
 184:         None
 185:     """
+186:     do_eigvecs=(eigvec_out is not False)
  __pyx_t_1 = (__pyx_v_eigvec_out != Py_False);
  __pyx_t_2 = __Pyx_PyBool_FromLong(__pyx_t_1); if (unlikely(!__pyx_t_2)) __PYX_ERR(0, 186, __pyx_L1_error)
  __Pyx_GOTREF(__pyx_t_2);
  __pyx_v_do_eigvecs = __pyx_t_2;
  __pyx_t_2 = 0;

+187:     if do_eigvecs:
  __pyx_t_1 = __Pyx_PyObject_IsTrue(__pyx_v_do_eigvecs); if (unlikely(__pyx_t_1 < 0)) __PYX_ERR(0, 187, __pyx_L1_error)
  if (__pyx_t_1) {
/* … */
    goto __pyx_L3;
  }

+188:         evec_slice=slice(dirichelet1,eigvec_out.shape[-1]-dirichelet2)

+231: def fem_solve(stiffness_matrix,load_matrix,load_vec,val_out,n,
/* Python wrapper */
static PyObject *__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_7fem_solve(PyObject *__pyx_self, PyObject *__pyx_args, PyObject *__pyx_kwds); /*proto*/
static char __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_6fem_solve[] = " Solve the linear matrix equation\n\n    For the mathematics/definitions of terms, see :ref:`FEM`.\n\n    Args:\n        stiffness_matrix: a stiffness matrix :math:`A` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_stiffness_matrix`\n        load_matrix: a load matrix :math:`M` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_load_matrix`\n        load_vec: the load vector :math:`b`, should be shape (len(zp))\n        val_out: an array into which to fill the solution, should be shape (len(zp))\n        n: dimension of the differential equation\n        dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.\n        *args,**kwargs: passed onto\n            `scipy.sparse.spsolve <https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve.html>`_\n            along with the main arguments `A` (stiffness) and `M@b` (load)\n\n    Returns:\n        the solution (same shape as `load_vec`)\n    ";
static PyMethodDef __pyx_mdef_9pynitride_4core_3fem_7fem_solve = {"fem_solve", (PyCFunction)(void*)(PyCFunctionWithKeywords)__pyx_pw_9pynitride_4core_3fem_7fem_solve, METH_VARARGS|METH_KEYWORDS, __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_6fem_solve};

+267: def fem_get_error(stiffness_matrix,load_matrix,load_vec,test,err_out,n,

static char __pyx_doc_9pynitride_4core_3fem_8fem_get_error[] = " Finds the signed error vector of the test solution.\n\n    The error is :math:`b-M^{-1}Ax`, where :math:`x` is the supplied test solution.\n    For the mathematics/definitions of terms, see :ref:`FEM`.\n\n    Args:\n        stiffness_matrix: a stiffness matrix :math:`A` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_stiffness_matrix`\n        load_matrix: a load matrix :math:`M` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_load_matrix`\n        load_vec: the load vector :math:`b`, should be shape (len(zp))\n        test: the test solution vector :math:`x`, should be shape (len(zp))\n        err_out: an array into which to fill the error, should be shape (len(zp))\n        n: dimension of the differential equation\n        dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.\n        *args,**kwargs: passed onto\n            `scipy.sparse.spsolve <https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve.html>`_\n            along with the main arguments `M` and `A@x`\n\n    Returns:\n        the error (same shape as `test`)\n    ";

 269:     """ Finds the signed error vector of the test solution.
 270: 
 271:     The error is :math:`b-M^{-1}Ax`, where :math:`x` is the supplied test solution.
 272:     For the mathematics/definitions of terms, see :ref:`FEM`.
 273: 
 274:     Args:
 275:         stiffness_matrix: a stiffness matrix :math:`A` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_stiffness_matrix`
 276:         load_matrix: a load matrix :math:`M` from :func:`pynitride.fem.assemble_load_matrix`
 277:         load_vec: the load vector :math:`b`, should be shape (len(zp))
 278:         test: the test solution vector :math:`x`, should be shape (len(zp))
 279:         err_out: an array into which to fill the error, should be shape (len(zp))
 280:         n: dimension of the differential equation
 281:         dirichelet1,dirichelet2 (bool): whether to treat boundary 1, 2 as Dirichelet (True, default) or Neumann.
 282:         *args,**kwargs: passed onto
 283:             `scipy.sparse.spsolve <https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve.html>`_
 284:             along with the main arguments `M` and `A@x`
 285: 
 286:     Returns:
 287:         the error (same shape as `test`)
 288:     """
